   251→```
   252→
   253→**Tablet (2 cards per row)**
   254→```
   255→┌────────────────────┐ ┌────────────────────┐
   256→│ Fleet Availability │ │ PM Compliance      │
   257→│ 87%     ↑ 2%      │ │ 94%     ↓ 1%      │
   258→└────────────────────┘ └────────────────────┘
   259→┌────────────────────┐ ┌────────────────────┐
   260→│ Budget Compliance  │ │ Mileage Balance    │

   264→
   265→**Mobile (Horizontal scroll carousel)**
   266→```
   267→┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
   268→│  ┌───────────┐  ┌───────────┐          │
   269→│  │ Fleet     │  │ PM        │  [...]   │
   270→│  │ Avail.    │  │ Compl.    │          │
   271→│  │ 87%  ↑2%  │  │ 94%  ↓1%  │          │
   272→│  └───────────┘  └───────────┘          │

   276→
   277→### 5.2 Charts
   278→
   279→**Desktop: Full interactive charts**
   280→
   281→**Tablet: Simplified charts with touch zoom**
   282→
   283→**Mobile: Sparklines + Tap to expand**
   284→
   285→```
   286→┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
   287→│ Man-Hour Utilization                   │

   292→```
   293→
   294→### 5.3 Date Pickers
   295→
   296→**Desktop: Calendar dropdown**
   297→
   298→**Mobile: Native date picker**
   299→- Use `<input type="date">` for system date picker
   300→- Respects user's locale and format preferences
   301→- Better keyboard support
   302→
   303→### 5.4 Filters
   304→
   305→**Desktop: Inline filter bar**
   306→```
   307→[Fleet Type ▼] [Status ▼] [PM Due ▼] [🔍 Search...]
   308→```
   309→
   310→**Mobile: Filter bottom sheet**
   311→```
   312→┌────────────────────────────────────────┐

   314→│            Filters                     │

   316→│ Fleet Type                             │
   317→│ ○ All  ● Kinki Sharyo  ○ Alstom       │
   318→│                                        │
   319→│ Status                                 │

   321→│ [ ] Maintenance  [ ] Out of Service   │
   322→│                                        │
   323→│ PM Due                                 │
   324→│ ○ All  ○ This Week  ○ This Month      │

   328→└────────────────────────────────────────┘
   329→```
   330→
   331→- Pull-down to dismiss
   332→- Result count preview
   333→- Sticky apply button
   334→
   335→### 5.5 Action Buttons
   336→
   337→**Desktop: Full text buttons**
   338→```
   339→[Schedule PM]  [View Details]  [Edit Assignment]
   340→```
   341→
   342→**Mobile: Icon buttons with labels on tap**
   343→```
   344→┌──────┐  ┌──────┐  ┌──────┐
   345→│  📅  │  │  👁️  │  │  ✏️  │
   346→│      │  │      │  │      │
   347→└──────┘  └──────┘  └──────┘
   348→Schedule   View     Edit
   349→```
   350→
   351→- Long-press for tooltip
   352→- Icon + short label below
   353→
   354→---
   355→
   356→## 6. Touch Interactions
   357→
   358→### 6.1 Touch Targets
   359→
   360→| Element | Minimum Size | Comfortable Size |
   361→|---------|--------------|------------------|
   362→| Buttons | 48x48px | 56x56px |
   363→| List items | 48px height | 56px height |
   364→| Form inputs | 48px height | 56px height |
   365→| Tab bar items | 48x48px | 64px wide |
   366→| Checkboxes | 24x24px (44px touch) | 32x32px |
   367→| Radio buttons | 24x24px (44px touch) | 32x32px |
   368→
   369→### 6.2 Gestures
   370→
   371→| Gesture | Action |
   372→|---------|--------|
   373→| Tap | Select, activate |
   374→| Long press | Context menu, drag start |
   375→| Swipe right | Mark as done, reveal actions |
   376→| Swipe left | Delete, archive |
   377→| Pull down | Refresh |
   378→| Pinch | Zoom (charts, maps) |
   379→| Two-finger scroll | Horizontal scroll in tables |
   380→
   381→### 6.3 Swipe Actions on List Items
   382→
   383→```
   384→┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
   385→│  ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ←     │
   386→│ ┌──────────────────────────────────┐   │
   387→│ │  [🗑️ Delete]    │ KS-045        │   │
   388→│ │                 │ PM in 3 days  │   │
   389→│ └──────────────────────────────────┘   │
   390→│                                        │
   391→│  → → → → → → → → → → → → → → → →     │
   392→│ ┌──────────────────────────────────┐   │
   393→│ │ KS-045        │    [✓ Done]     │   │
   394→│ │ PM in 3 days  │                 │   │
   395→│ └──────────────────────────────────┘   │
   396→└────────────────────────────────────────┘
   397→```
   398→
   399→---
   400→
   401→## 7. Offline-First Design
   402→
   403→### 7.1 App Shell Architecture
   404→
   405→```
   406→┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
   407→│ [Logo]                     [🔔][👤]    │  ← Cached (Shell)
   408→├────────────────────────────────────────┤
   409→│                                        │
   410→│  ┌────────────────────────────────┐   │
   411→│  │                                │   │
   412→│  │     DYNAMIC CONTENT            │   │  ← Fetched/Cached
   413→│  │     (API data)                 │   │
   414→│  │                                │   │
   415→│  └────────────────────────────────┘   │
   416→│                                        │
   417→├────────────────────────────────────────┤
   418→│  🏠     🚂     📅     📊     ☰       │  ← Cached (Shell)
   419→└────────────────────────────────────────┘
   420→```
   421→
   422→### 7.2 Caching Strategy
   423→
   424→| Data Type | Cache Strategy | Staleness |
   425→|-----------|----------------|-----------|
   426→| App shell (HTML/CSS/JS) | Cache first | Immediate update |
   427→| Train list | Stale-while-revalidate | 5 minutes |
   428→| Mileage data | Stale-while-revalidate | 1 hour |
   429→| PM schedules | Network first, cache fallback | Real-time |
   430→| User preferences | Cache first | On change |
   431→| Images/icons | Cache first | Weekly update |
   432→
   433→### 7.3 Offline Indicators
   434→
   435→**Header offline indicator:**
   436→```
   437→┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
   438→│ ⚡ Offline - Changes will sync when... │
   439→└────────────────────────────────────────┘
   440→```
   441→
   442→**Per-item sync status:**
   443→```
   444→┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
   445→│ KS-045                   ⏳ Syncing... │
   446→│ Mileage updated to 46,234 km          │
   447→└────────────────────────────────────────┘
   448→
   449→┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
   450→│ KS-046                   ✓ Synced      │
   451→│ Assignment confirmed                   │
   452→└────────────────────────────────────────┘
   453→```
   454→
   455→### 7.4 Optimistic UI Updates
   456→
   457→```
   458→User Action: Record mileage for KS-045
   459→
   460→1. Immediate UI update (optimistic)
   461→   ┌────────────────────────────────────┐
   462→   │ ✓ Mileage recorded: 46,234 km     │
   463→   └────────────────────────────────────┘
   464→
   465→2. Background sync attempt
   466→   - If online: Confirm with server
   467→   - If offline: Queue for later sync
   468→
   469→3. On sync failure: Show retry option
   470→   ┌────────────────────────────────────┐
   471→   │ ⚠ Failed to sync mileage         │
   472→   │ [Retry]  [Discard]                │
   473→   └────────────────────────────────────┘
   474→```
   475→
   476→### 7.5 Offline-Available Features
   477→
   478→| Feature | Offline Support | Notes |
   479→|---------|-----------------|-------|
   480→| View train list | ✓ Full | Cached data |
   481→| View train details | ✓ Full | Cached data |
   482→| Record daily mileage | ✓ Queue | Syncs when online |
   483→| View PM schedule | ✓ Full | Cached data |
   484→| Create assignment | ✓ Queue | Syncs when online |
   485→| View notifications | ✓ Partial | Cached recent |
   486→| Generate reports | ✗ No | Requires server |
   487→| Run simulations | ✗ No | Requires server |
   488→
   489→---
   490→
   491→## 8. Field Worker Optimizations
   492→
   493→### 8.1 Environmental Considerations
   494→
   495→Based on railway maintenance UX research:
   496→
   497→| Challenge | Solution |
   498→|-----------|----------|
   499→| Poor lighting | High contrast mode, larger fonts |
   500→| Gloves | Larger touch targets (64px+) |
   501→| Dirty screens | Simple swipe gestures, avoid multi-touch |
   502→| Attention span | Progressive disclosure, highlight key info |
   503→| Eyeglasses | Avoid small text (<16px) |
   504→| Outdoor glare | Dark mode option, high contrast |
   505→
   506→### 8.2 High Contrast Mode
   507→
   508→```css
   509→@media (prefers-contrast: more) {

   511→    --bg-primary: #000000;
   512→    --bg-secondary: #1a1a1a;
   513→    --text-primary: #ffffff;
   514→    --text-secondary: #e0e0e0;
   515→    --accent-color: #ffcc00;
   516→    --error-color: #ff4444;
   517→    --success-color: #44ff44;
   518→  }
   519→}
   520→```
   521→
   522→### 8.3 Quick Actions for Field Workers
   523→
   524→**Train check-in card (optimized for gloves):**

   534→│  │                                  │ │
   535→│  │      or enter manually          │ │
   536→│  └──────────────────────────────────┘ │
   537→│                                        │
   538→│  ┌────────────────────────────────┐   │
   539→│  │                                │   │
   540→│  │        ✓ CHECK IN              │   │  ← 64px height
   541→│  │                                │   │
   542→│  └────────────────────────────────┘   │
   543→│                                        │
   544→│  Current Mileage: 46,234 km           │
   545→│  Last Checked: 2 hours ago            │
   546→│                                        │
   547→└────────────────────────────────────────┘
   548→```
   549→
   550→### 8.4 Voice Input Support
   551→
   552→```
   553→┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
   554→│ Enter Mileage                          │
   555→│                                        │
   556→│ ┌────────────────────────────────────┐│

   561→│                                        │

   563→└────────────────────────────────────────┘
   564→```
   565→
   566→---
   567→
   568→## 9. Role-Based Mobile Views
   569→
   570→### 9.1 Operations Role
   571→
   572→**Primary tasks:**
   573→- View daily schedule
   574→- Assign/reassign trains
   575→- Check fleet availability
   576→- Respond to alerts
   577→

   580→┌────────────────────────────────────────┐

   587→│ │ 06:00 - 14:00              FIT    ││
   588→│ └────────────────────────────────────┘│
   589→│                                        │

   602→
   603→### 9.2 Maintenance Role

   684→## 10. Progressive Web App (PWA)
   685→

   719→### 10.2 Service Worker Strategy
   720→
   721→```javascript
   722→// Caching strategies
   723→const strategies = {
   724→  // App shell - cache first
   725→  shell: 'CacheFirst',
   726→
   727→  // API data - stale while revalidate
   728→  api: 'StaleWhileRevalidate',
   729→
   730→  // Images - cache first with expiration

   753→
   754→## 11. Performance Optimization
   755→
   756→### 11.1 Performance Budgets
   757→
   758→| Metric | Target | Mobile 3G |
   759→|--------|--------|-----------|
   760→| First Contentful Paint | <1.5s | <3s |
   761→| Largest Contentful Paint | <2.5s | <4s |
   762→| Time to Interactive | <3.5s | <7s |
   763→| Total Blocking Time | <200ms | <600ms |
   764→| Cumulative Layout Shift | <0.1 | <0.1 |
   765→| Bundle size (gzipped) | <200KB | <200KB |
   766→
   767→### 11.2 Optimization Techniques
   768→
   769→| Technique | Implementation |
   770→|-----------|----------------|
   771→| Code splitting | Route-based lazy loading |
   772→| Image optimization | WebP format, srcset |
   773→| Critical CSS | Inline above-fold styles |
   774→| Prefetching | Priority routes |
   775→| Skeleton screens | Show immediately while loading |
   776→| Virtual scrolling | For long lists (128 trains) |
   777→
   778→### 11.3 Skeleton Loading States
   779→
   780→```
   781→┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
   782→│ ████████████████                       │
   783→│ Kinki Sharyo                           │
   784→├────────────────────────────────────────┤
   785→│ ████████   │ ████████████  │ ████      │
   786→│            │               │           │
   787→├────────────────────────────────────────┤
   788→│ ████████████████████████               │
   789→│ ████████████████████                   │
   790→├────────────────────────────────────────┤
   791→│ ████████████        ████████████       │
   792→└────────────────────────────────────────┘
   793→```
   794→
   795→---
   796→
   797→## 12. Accessibility (Mobile)
   798→
   799→### 12.1 Screen Reader Support
   800→
   801→```html
   802→<!-- Train card -->
   803→<article
   804→  role="article"
   805→  aria-label="Train KS-045, Kinki Sharyo fleet, 95% PM window, Warning status"
   806→>
   807→  <h3 id="train-title-045">KS-045</h3>
   808→  <p aria-describedby="train-title-045">Kinki Sharyo</p>
   809→
   810→  <div role="meter" aria-valuenow="95" aria-valuemin="0" aria-valuemax="100">
   811→    PM Window: 95%
   812→  </div>
   813→
   814→  <button aria-label="View details for train KS-045">
   815→    View Details
   816→  </button>
   817→</article>
   818→```
   819→
   820→### 12.2 Focus Management
   821→
   822→- Trap focus in modals/bottom sheets
   823→- Return focus to trigger element on close
   824→- Visible focus indicators (4px outline)
   825→- Skip-to-content link
   826→
   827→### 12.3 Reduced Motion
   828→
   829→```css
   830→@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
   831→  *,
   832→  *::before,
   833→  *::after {
   834→    animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
   835→    animation-iteration-count: 1 !important;
   836→    transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
   837→  }
   838→}
   839→```
   840→
   841→---
   842→
   843→## 13. Testing Checklist
   844→
   845→### 13.1 Device Testing Matrix
   846→
   847→| Device | OS | Browser | Priority |
   848→|--------|----|---------|---------|
   849→| iPhone 14 Pro | iOS 17 | Safari | High |
   850→| iPhone SE | iOS 17 | Safari | High |
   851→| iPad Pro 12.9" | iPadOS 17 | Safari | High |
   852→| Samsung Galaxy S23 | Android 14 | Chrome | High |
   853→| Pixel 7 | Android 14 | Chrome | Medium |
   854→| iPad Air | iPadOS 17 | Safari | Medium |
   855→| Samsung Tab S9 | Android 14 | Chrome | Medium |
   856→
   857→### 13.2 Test Scenarios
   858→
   859→| Scenario | Test Method |
   860→|----------|-------------|
   861→| Offline functionality | Airplane mode toggle |
   862→| Slow network | Chrome DevTools throttling |
   863→| Touch targets | Manual testing with fingers |
   864→| Orientation change | Device rotation |
   865→| Keyboard navigation | External keyboard |
   866→| Screen reader | VoiceOver / TalkBack |
   867→| PWA install | Add to home screen |
   868→| Background sync | Network restoration |
   869→
   870→---
   871→
   872→## 14. Gestalt Principles (Mobile)
   873→
   874→### 14.1 Proximity
   875→- Group related actions in bottom sheets
   876→- Cluster form fields logically
   877→- Maintain consistent spacing
   878→
   879→### 14.2 Similarity
   880→- Consistent card styling across views
   881→- Uniform icon language
   882→- Same color coding for status
   883→
   884→### 14.3 Enclosure
   885→- Cards with clear boundaries
   886→- Bottom sheet modal frames
   887→- Section separators
   888→
   889→### 14.4 Continuity
   890→- Smooth scroll animations
   891→- Gesture continuity (swipe flow)
   892→- Consistent navigation direction
   893→
   894→### 14.5 Figure-Ground
   895→- Elevated bottom sheets (shadow)
   896→- Modal overlay dimming
   897→- Active tab highlighting
   898→
   899→### 14.6 Common Fate
   900→- List items scroll together
   901→- Synchronized pull-to-refresh
   902→- Batch selection animations
   903→
   904→---
   905→
   906→## 15. Unknown Unknowns (Risks & Considerations)
   907→
   908→### Identified During Design
   909→
   910→1. **Data usage concerns**: Field workers may have limited data plans
   911→2. **Battery consumption**: Background sync impact on battery
   912→3. **Device fragmentation**: Android device diversity
   913→4. **Network reliability**: Rural/underground coverage gaps
   914→5. **PWA limitations**: iOS restrictions on background processing
   915→6. **Biometric auth**: Device-specific implementation differences
   916→7. **Camera quality**: QR scanning in poor lighting
   917→8. **Screen size extremes**: Very large tablets, foldables
   918→9. **Rugged device support**: Industrial-grade tablets
   919→10. **Multi-window support**: Split-screen on tablets
   920→11. **Handedness**: Left-handed users and navigation
   921→12. **Accessibility zoom**: Text scaling impact on layout
   922→13. **Dark mode sync**: System vs app preference
   923→14. **Update mechanism**: PWA update prompts
   924→15. **Storage limits**: Browser cache limits
   925→16. **Cross-device sync**: Session continuity
   926→17. **Print from mobile**: Report printing support
   927→18. **External keyboard**: Tablet with keyboard use case
   928→19. **Landscape lock**: Specific views that need landscape
   929→20. **Deep linking**: Navigation from push notifications
   930→
   931→---
   932→
   933→## 16. Implementation Checklist
   934→
   935→### Phase 1: Foundation
   936→- [ ] Set up responsive breakpoints
   937→- [ ] Implement mobile navigation
   938→- [ ] Create base card components
   939→- [ ] Set up PWA manifest
   940→
   941→### Phase 2: Core Features
   942→- [ ] Mobile dashboard view
   943→- [ ] Train list (cards)
   944→- [ ] Train detail view
   945→- [ ] Basic offline support
   946→
   947→### Phase 3: Field Worker
   948→- [ ] Quick check-in flow
   949→- [ ] Mileage entry (offline)
   950→- [ ] Task list view
   951→- [ ] QR code scanning
   952→
   953→### Phase 4: Advanced
   954→- [ ] Full offline sync
   955→- [ ] Push notifications
   956→- [ ] Background sync
   957→- [ ] Role-based views
   958→
   959→### Phase 5: Polish
   960→- [ ] Performance optimization
   961→- [ ] Accessibility audit
   962→- [ ] Device testing
   963→- [ ] PWA install flow
   964→
   965→---
   966→
   967→## 17. Change Log
   968→
   969→| Version | Date | Changes |
   970→|---------|------|---------|
   971→| 1.0 | 2026-01-08 | Initial specification |
   972→
   973→---
   974→

# Mobile Responsive Design UX Specification

## MILEAGE-UX-010 - Rolling Stock Mileage Management

**Version:** 1.0
**Last Updated:** 2026-01-08
**Status:** Complete

---

## 1. Overview

### Purpose
Define mobile-responsive design patterns for the Rolling Stock Mileage Management platform, enabling field access for operations and maintenance personnel while maintaining full functionality across all device sizes.

### Scope
- 128 trains (49 Kinki Sharyo + 79 Alstom)
- RFQ Section 6: "The web-based software shall be compatible with mainstream web browsers and shall be accessible on desktops and mobile devices."
- Support for Operations, Maintenance, and Management roles on mobile
- Offline-first capability for field environments

### Design Principles
1. **Mobile-First**: Design for smallest screens first, then enhance
2. **Progressive Enhancement**: Basic functionality works everywhere
3. **Touch-Friendly**: Minimum 48px touch targets
4. **Offline-First**: Core features available without connectivity
5. **Performance**: Fast load times on 3G networks
6. **Accessibility**: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance on all devices

### Sources Referenced
- [Hicron: Fleet Management Dashboard Design](https://hicronsoftware.com/blog/fleet-management-dashboard-design/)
- [Volpis: UX Design of Fleet Management Apps](https://volpis.com/blog/user-experience-design-of-fleet-management-apps/)
- [Standard Beagle: Fleet Tablet App Design](https://standardbeagle.com/case-studies/fleet-tablet-app-design/)
- [Medium: Railway Maintenance SaaS UX Design](https://medium.com/@kirillgorban/smart-maintenance-saas-solution-for-railroad-switches-ux-design-approach-cd2c9851af4c)
- [Google: PWA Offline-First Architecture](https://developers.google.com/codelabs/pwa-training/pwa03--going-offline)
- [MDN: Offline and Background Operation](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Progressive_web_apps/Guides/Offline_and_background_operation)

---

## 2. Responsive Breakpoints

### 2.1 Breakpoint Definitions

| Breakpoint | Width | Target Devices | Layout |
|------------|-------|----------------|--------|
| xs (Mobile) | <576px | iPhone SE, small phones | Single column |
| sm (Mobile Large) | 576-767px | iPhone 14, large phones | Single column |
| md (Tablet Portrait) | 768-991px | iPad portrait | 2-column |
| lg (Tablet Landscape) | 992-1199px | iPad landscape, small laptops | 3-column |
| xl (Desktop) | 1200-1399px | Standard monitors | Full layout |
| xxl (Desktop Large) | >=1400px | Large monitors, 4K | Full layout |

### 2.2 CSS Variables

```css
:root {
  /* Breakpoints */
  --breakpoint-xs: 0;
  --breakpoint-sm: 576px;
  --breakpoint-md: 768px;
  --breakpoint-lg: 992px;
  --breakpoint-xl: 1200px;
  --breakpoint-xxl: 1400px;

  /* Touch targets */
  --touch-target-min: 48px;
  --touch-target-comfortable: 56px;

  /* Spacing */
  --spacing-xs: 4px;
  --spacing-sm: 8px;
  --spacing-md: 16px;
  --spacing-lg: 24px;
  --spacing-xl: 32px;

  /* Typography (mobile-first) */
  --font-size-base: 16px;
  --font-size-sm: 14px;
  --font-size-xs: 12px;
  --font-size-lg: 18px;
  --font-size-xl: 20px;
  --font-size-h1: 24px;
  --font-size-h2: 20px;
  --font-size-h3: 18px;
}
```

---

## 3. Navigation Patterns

### 3.1 Desktop Navigation (>=992px)


## 7. Offline-First Design

### 7.1 App Shell Architecture

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ [Logo]                     [🔔][👤]    │  ← Cached (Shell)
├────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                        │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │                                │   │
│  │     DYNAMIC CONTENT            │   │  ← Fetched/Cached
│  │     (API data)                 │   │
│  │                                │   │
│  └────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                        │
├────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  🏠     🚂     📅     📊     ☰       │  ← Cached (Shell)
└────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 7.2 Caching Strategy

| Data Type | Cache Strategy | Staleness |
|-----------|----------------|-----------|
| App shell (HTML/CSS/JS) | Cache first | Immediate update |
| Train list | Stale-while-revalidate | 5 minutes |
| Mileage data | Stale-while-revalidate | 1 hour |
| PM schedules | Network first, cache fallback | Real-time |
| User preferences | Cache first | On change |
| Images/icons | Cache first | Weekly update |

### 7.3 Offline Indicators

**Header offline indicator:**
```
┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ⚡ Offline - Changes will sync when... │
└────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Per-item sync status:**
```
┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ KS-045                   ⏳ Syncing... │
│ Mileage updated to 46,234 km          │
└────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ KS-046                   ✓ Synced      │
│ Assignment confirmed                   │
└────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 7.4 Optimistic UI Updates

```
User Action: Record mileage for KS-045

1. Immediate UI update (optimistic)
   ┌────────────────────────────────────┐
   │ ✓ Mileage recorded: 46,234 km     │
   └────────────────────────────────────┘

2. Background sync attempt
   - If online: Confirm with server
   - If offline: Queue for later sync

3. On sync failure: Show retry option
   ┌────────────────────────────────────┐
   │ ⚠ Failed to sync mileage         │
   │ [Retry]  [Discard]                │
   └────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 7.5 Offline-Available Features

| Feature | Offline Support | Notes |
|---------|-----------------|-------|
| View train list | ✓ Full | Cached data |
| View train details | ✓ Full | Cached data |
| Record daily mileage | ✓ Queue | Syncs when online |
| View PM schedule | ✓ Full | Cached data |
| Create assignment | ✓ Queue | Syncs when online |
| View notifications | ✓ Partial | Cached recent |
| Generate reports | ✗ No | Requires server |
| Run simulations | ✗ No | Requires server |

## 8. Field Worker Optimizations

### 8.1 Environmental Considerations

Based on railway maintenance UX research:

| Challenge | Solution |
|-----------|----------|
| Poor lighting | High contrast mode, larger fonts |
| Gloves | Larger touch targets (64px+) |
| Dirty screens | Simple swipe gestures, avoid multi-touch |
| Attention span | Progressive disclosure, highlight key info |
| Eyeglasses | Avoid small text (<16px) |
| Outdoor glare | Dark mode option, high contrast |

### 8.2 High Contrast Mode

```css
@media (prefers-contrast: more) {
  :root {
    --bg-primary: #000000;
    --bg-secondary: #1a1a1a;
    --text-primary: #ffffff;
    --text-secondary: #e0e0e0;
    --accent-color: #ffcc00;
    --error-color: #ff4444;
    --success-color: #44ff44;
  }
}
```

### 10.2 Service Worker Strategy

```javascript
// Caching strategies
const strategies = {
  // App shell - cache first
  shell: 'CacheFirst',

  // API data - stale while revalidate
  api: 'StaleWhileRevalidate',

  // Images - cache first with expiration
  images: 'CacheFirst',

  // Real-time data - network first

};
```

## 11. Performance Optimization

### 11.1 Performance Budgets

| Metric | Target | Mobile 3G |
|--------|--------|-----------|
| First Contentful Paint | <1.5s | <3s |
| Largest Contentful Paint | <2.5s | <4s |
| Time to Interactive | <3.5s | <7s |

| Cumulative Layout Shift | <0.1 | <0.1 |
| Bundle size (gzipped) | <200KB | <200KB |

| Technique | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| Code splitting | Route-based lazy loading |
| Image optimization | WebP format, srcset |
| Critical CSS | Inline above-fold styles |
| Prefetching | Priority routes |
| Skeleton screens | Show immediately while loading |
| Virtual scrolling | For long lists (128 trains) |

```css
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {

    animation-iteration-count: 1 !important;
    transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
  }
}
```

## 14. Gestalt Principles (Mobile)

### 14.1 Proximity
- Group related actions in bottom sheets
- Cluster form fields logically
- Maintain consistent spacing

### 14.2 Similarity
- Consistent card styling across views
- Uniform icon language
- Same color coding for status

### 14.3 Enclosure
- Cards with clear boundaries
- Bottom sheet modal frames
- Section separators

### 14.4 Continuity
- Smooth scroll animations
- Gesture continuity (swipe flow)
- Consistent navigation direction

### 14.5 Figure-Ground
- Elevated bottom sheets (shadow)
- Modal overlay dimming
- Active tab highlighting

### 14.6 Common Fate
- List items scroll together
- Synchronized pull-to-refresh
- Batch selection animations

---

## 15. Unknown Unknowns (Risks & Considerations)

### Identified During Design

1. **Data usage concerns**: Field workers may have limited data plans
2. **Battery consumption**: Background sync impact on battery
3. **Device fragmentation**: Android device diversity
4. **Network reliability**: Rural/underground coverage gaps
5. **PWA limitations**: iOS restrictions on background processing
6. **Biometric auth**: Device-specific implementation differences
7. **Camera quality**: QR scanning in poor lighting
8. **Screen size extremes**: Very large tablets, foldables
9. **Rugged device support**: Industrial-grade tablets
10. **Multi-window support**: Split-screen on tablets
11. **Handedness**: Left-handed users and navigation
12. **Accessibility zoom**: Text scaling impact on layout
13. **Dark mode sync**: System vs app preference
14. **Update mechanism**: PWA update prompts
15. **Storage limits**: Browser cache limits
16. **Cross-device sync**: Session continuity
17. **Print from mobile**: Report printing support
18. **External keyboard**: Tablet with keyboard use case
19. **Landscape lock**: Specific views that need landscape
20. **Deep linking**: Navigation from push notifications

---

Here are the search results for PatternFly 6 React design system components in 2025:

## PatternFly 6 Overview

PatternFly is Red Hat's open source design system. It consists of components, documentation, and code for building enterprise applications at scale.

## Q2 2025 Release Updates

For the Q2 2025 PatternFly release, progress was made in key initiatives, including PatternFly 6 migration enablement, component animations, React 19 support, and the continued evolution of PatternFly AI.(no content)For the Q2 2025 PatternFly release, the initiative to support React 19 across PatternFly was completed, and significant progress was made towards finalizing component animations.(no content)PatternFly now includes support for React 19 across all components and extensions, enabling users to benefit from new React features and performance enhancements. This update is non-breaking, and PatternFly 6 will continue to support React 17 and 18 in line with their support policy.

## React Library Packages

PatternFly's React library contains a collection of components that you can use to build interfaces with consistent markup, styling, and behavior. It is composed of a number of React packages, including @patternfly/react-core for most React core components.

Additional packages include:
- @patternfly/react-charts: PatternFly charting components.
- @patternfly/react-icons: PatternFly icons as React components. @patternfly/react-tokens: PatternFly's CSS variables, transformed into JavaScript objects for use with React.

## PatternFly 6 Design Token System

The new design token system in PatternFly 6 changes variable names across PatternFly. Any existing CSS overrides will be targeting outdated styles and will no longer work, so they must be updated or removed.(no content)PatternFly 6 uses rem units for global breakpoint design tokens, rather than pixels. If you have previously implemented any breakpoint logic based on a pixel value, you will need to account for the fact that PatternFly 6 breakpoint tokens use rems.

## Installation

Using npm, run the following command to install: npm install @patternfly/react-core --save. Or using yarn, run: yarn add @patternfly/react-core.

## Migration Resources

To assist with naming updates, PatternFly offers 3 codemods: class-name-updater (automatically identifies and renames classes), tokens-update (updates global CSS variables in React code), and css-vars-updater (updates CSS variables in non-React files).

For comprehensive release notes, they can be found on GitHub, in @patternfly/patternfly and @patternfly/react-core.

Based on my search, I found information about TanStack Table and PatternFly separately, but there doesn't appear to be a specific, direct integration between TanStack Table and PatternFly documented in the search results. Here's what I found:

## TanStack Table

TanStack Table is a Headless UI library for building powerful tables & datagrids for TS/JS, React, Vue, Solid, Qwik, and Svelte.(no content)It's a headless table library for building powerful datagrids with full control over markup, styles, and behavior, with a framework-agnostic core with bindings for React, Vue & Solid.(no content)TanStack Table allows you to supercharge your tables or build a datagrid from scratch for TS/JS, React, Vue, Solid, Svelte, Qwik, Angular, and Lit while retaining 100% control over markup and styles.

## PatternFly Table

PatternFly's Table component displays large data sets in a simple grid with column headers, and lives in its own package at @patternfly/react-table. The Table component takes an explicit and declarative approach, and its implementation closely mirrors that of an HTML table.(no content)PatternFly's Table component allows you to compose the table by nesting the relevant Thead, Tbody, Tr, Th and Td components within it.

## Integration Approach

While there's no specific TanStack Table + PatternFly integration documented in the search results, headless table libraries like TanStack Table typically supply you with functions, state, utilities and event listeners to build your own table markup or attach to existing table markups.

This means you could theoretically use TanStack Table's logic and state management with PatternFly's table components (`Table`, `Thead`, `Tbody`, `Tr`, `Th`, `Td`) for styling. The approach would be similar to how developers integrate TanStack Table with Fluent UI - since TanStack Table is a headless UI library that provides functionality without imposing styling constraints, it's perfect for integration with design systems.

Here are the best practices for React TypeScript Vite project structure in 2025:

## Project Setup

In 2025, Vite continues to simplify React app creation with the most streamlined experience yet. The quickest way to kickstart your project is through Vite's official scaffold: `npm create vite@latest my-react-app -- --template react`.(no content)You'll find a straightforward setup featuring an index.html at the root and a src/ directory containing your main React entry file (main.jsx or main.tsx).

## Recommended Folder Structure

For a React project in 2025, a well-organized folder structure is essential for maintainability, scalability, and ease of collaboration. The structure should be modular, flexible, and adaptable to different types of projects, whether you're building a small app or a large-scale enterprise application.

### Root Level Structure
At the root of your project, you should have these typical files and directories: `/public/`, `/src/`, `/assets/`, `.gitignore`, `package.json`, `README.md`, `tsconfig.json` (for TypeScript projects), `vite.config.js` (for Vite projects), and `.eslintrc.json`.

### Recommended src/ Structure
Organize your project with this recommended structure:
```
src/
├── assets/       # Static assets
├── components/   # Reusable components
├── hooks/        # Custom hooks
├── pages/        # Page components
├── services/     # API services
├── styles/       # Global styles
├── types/        # TypeScript types
```

## Feature-Based Organization

Once your app starts growing, group files by features or domains, not by file types. By 'feature' or 'domain', grouping means all code related to a business unit — like auth, dashboard, or profile:
```
src/
├── features/
│   ├── auth/
│   │   ├── Auth.tsx
│   │   └── auth.service.ts
│   └── dashboard/
│       ├── Dashboard.tsx
│       └── dashboard.api.ts
├── components/
│   └── Navbar.tsx
├── App.tsx
└── main.tsx
```

## Naming Conventions

Best practices include: kebab-case files, PascalCase components, camelCase everything else, and a modular folder structure.

## Key Configuration Tools

To enforce code standards, set up ESLint with plugins like eslint-plugin-react and eslint-plugin-typescript. Vite's built-in support for ESLint means instant feedback during development, catching inconsistencies early.(no content)A complete setup includes configuring essential tools like ESLint, Prettier, and environment variables, plus advanced features like code splitting, aliasing, and optimizing your project for production.

## Why Vite?

Vite stands out in 2025 by using a radically different architecture compared to traditional bundlers. During development, instead of bundling all your code upfront, Vite serves your React app as native ES modules directly in the browser. This approach significantly reduces startup time because only necessary files are loaded when needed. Additionally, Vite leverages esbuild or SWC (a modern, Rust-based transformer) to compile JSX and TypeScript files almost instantly.(no content)SWC is a JavaScript/TypeScript compiler used in Vite for transforming JavaScript and TypeScript code. It is a fast and low-level compiler written in Rust to provide faster build times and better performance.

## Best Practice Summary

"A folder structure isn't just about organizing files — it's about creating a system that scales with your code and your team. Start simple, refactor as you grow, and always keep readability and maintainability in mind."


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about PatternFly react-charts and Victory charts:

## PatternFly React Charts

PatternFly provides charting components through the `@patternfly/react-charts` package.(no content)PatternFly React charts are based on the Victory chart library, along with additional functionality, custom components, and theming for PatternFly.

### Installation

You can install it using:
```
yarn add @patternfly/react-charts
yarn add victory
```
Note that Victory is now an optional peer dependency, allowing future chart libraries to be installed without including Victory dependencies and vice versa. You may choose to install the single "victory" package to cover all features, or install packages based on the features used in your app (e.g., "victory-core", "victory-tooltip", etc.).

### Usage

It's strongly advised to use the PatternFly Base CSS in your whole project. Components can be imported from `@patternfly/react-charts/victory`:

```javascript
import '@patternfly/react-core/dist/styles/base.css';
import { Area } from '@patternfly/react-charts/victory';
```

There is no component level CSS to import. The library makes use of the babel plugin from @patternfly/react-styles to enable providing the CSS alongside the components, which removes the need for consumers to use style/css/sass-loaders.

### Available Chart Types

PatternFly provides a collection of React based components you can use to build PatternFly patterns with consistent markup, styling, and behavior. The examples are based on the Victory chart library with additional functionality, custom components, and theming for PatternFly.

Available chart components include:
- Area charts
- Bar charts
- Line charts
- Box plot charts
- Bullet charts
- Donut/Pie charts
- And more

### Victory Library

Victory is a collection of composable React components for building interactive data visualizations - an ecosystem of composable React components.(no content)As of victory@34.0.0, Victory requires React version 16.3.0 or above.(no content)PatternFly is Red Hat's open source design system consisting of components, documentation, and code for building enterprise applications at scale.

Links: [{"title":"Build a simple component | Storybook Tutorials","url":"https://storybook.js.org/tutorials/intro-to-storybook/react/en/simple-component/"},{"title":"Building a Component Library with React, Typescript, and Storybook: A Comprehensive Guide | by Ashish Kachhadiya | Simform Engineering | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/simform-engineering/building-a-component-library-with-react-typescript-and-storybook-a-comprehensive-guide-ba189accdaf5"},{"title":"React UI Components: How to Build with TypeScript and Storybook","url":"https://www.dhiwise.com/post/build-react-ui-component-with-typescript-and-storybook"},{"title":"Storybook 8","url":"https://storybook.js.org/blog/storybook-8/"},{"title":"TypeScript | Storybook docs","url":"https://storybook.js.org/docs/configure/integration/typescript"},{"title":"Writing stories in TypeScript | Storybook docs","url":"https://storybook.js.org/docs/writing-stories/typescript"},{"title":"How to Implement Storybook in React + TypeScript + Material UI application - DEV Community","url":"https://dev.to/sanchithasr/how-to-implement-storybook-in-react-typescript-material-ui-application-4om7"},{"title":"Storybook-Driven Development for Design Systems and Component Libraries | by Loraine Garutti | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@lorainegarutti/storybook-driven-development-for-design-systems-and-component-libraries-ab9fab1a3a44"},{"title":"Documentation of components in React with TypeScript using Storybook (version 8) - DEV Community","url":"https://dev.to/griseduardo/documentation-of-components-in-react-with-typescript-using-storybook-version-8-ke7"},{"title":"Storybook TypeScript Setup: A Comprehensive Guide — xjavascript.com","url":"https://www.xjavascript.com/blog/storybook-typescript-setup/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Storybook 8 with React TypeScript component-driven development best practices:

## Storybook 8 Key Features for React + TypeScript

Storybook is the industry standard UI tool for building, testing, and documenting components and pages. It's used by thousands of teams globally, integrates with all major JavaScript frameworks, and combines with most leading design and developer tools.

### Storybook 8 Improvements
Storybook 8 introduces major improvements to controls autogeneration for React and Vue projects. For React users, Storybook 8 switches to a significantly faster docgen library: react-docgen. Unlike the previous TypeScript-based solution, react-docgen produces a shallower analysis that is way faster and good enough for virtually all components, resulting in up to 50% faster React Storybook startup times.(no content)The full TypeScript solution is still available as a fallback for anyone who needs it.

## TypeScript Best Practices

### Story Typing with Meta and StoryObj
When writing stories, there are two aspects that are helpful to type. The first is the component meta, which describes and configures the component and its stories. In a CSF file, this is the default export. The second is the stories themselves. Storybook provides utility types for each of these, named Meta and StoryObj.

### Using the `satisfies` Operator
Assuming that you're working on a project that uses TypeScript 4.9+, you can update your component stories to use the new satisfies operator to ensure stricter type checking for your component stories. Out of the box, Storybook supports the satisfies operator for almost every framework already using TypeScript version 4.9 or higher.

### Defining Component Types
As we continue to build out our components, we can specify the shape of the data that the component expects by defining a TypeScript type. This way, we can catch errors early and ensure the component is used correctly when adding more complexity.

## Component-Driven Development Best Practices

### Building Components in Isolation
Getting started building components in isolation is easy and fast. We can expect to produce a higher-quality UI with fewer bugs and more polish because it's possible to dig in and test every possible state.

### Story Organization
There are two basic levels of organization in Storybook: the component and its child stories. Think of each story as a permutation of a component. You can have as many stories per component as you need.

### Using CSF3 Format
To define stories, use Component Story Format 3 (CSF3) to build out each test case. This format is designed to build out each of our test cases in a concise way. By exporting an object containing each component state, we can define our tests more intuitively and author and reuse stories more efficiently.

### Testing Integration
Storybook provides the possibility to test events with mocked interactions and assertions inside play functions because it's written using a Storybook-instrumented version of Testing Library and Jest. It helps improve developer experience showing defects on stories rendering. Execute tests with test runner to catch broken stories (even those without a play function).

### Accessibility Testing
Test components with tools like aXe or Storybook's accessibility add-on to ensure that they meet accessibility standards. By following these best practices, you ensure your React UI components are robust, reusable, and scalable.

### Additional Recommendations
Leverage TypeScript's type safety for enhanced component development and prop documentation within Storybook. Consider using the Material UI theme provider in your preview.tsx to ensure consistent styling across your stories.(no content)Use Storybook in combination with testing libraries like Jest and React Testing Library. Write tests for your components and use stories to visualize the expected behavior.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

/**
 * Mileage Management Types - Public API
 *
 * Re-exports all types for the Rolling Stock Mileage Management System.
 * Supports 128 trains (49 Kinki Sharyo + 79 Alstom) over 12-year contract.
 */

// Train and Fleet types
export type {
  FleetType,
  FitnessStatus,
  TrainStatus,
  PMLevel,
  Train,
  NextPMDue,
  MaintenanceHistoryEntry,
  FleetSummary,
  FleetComposition,
  FleetConfiguration,
} from './train';

export {
  DEFAULT_PM_INTERVALS,
  DEFAULT_FLEET_CONFIGS,
  PM_LEVELS_ORDER,
  generateTrainId,
  getNextPMLevel,
  calculatePMDueDate,
} from './train';

// Mileage record types
export type {
  MileageRecord,
  MileageWindow,
  MileageForecast,
  MileageProjection,
  MileageVariance,
  MileageWarning,
  FleetMileageDistribution,
  MileageBand,
  DailyFleetMileageSummary,
  MileageThresholdConfig,
} from './mileage';

export {
  DEFAULT_MILEAGE_THRESHOLDS,
  DEFAULT_PM_MILEAGE_THRESHOLDS,
  getMileageWindowStatus,
  getVarianceStatus,
  getWarningSeverity,
} from './mileage';

// Maintenance plan types
export type {
  MaintenanceCategory,
  MaintenanceStatus,
  MaintenanceActivity,
  SparePart,
  MaintenancePlan,
  ScheduledMaintenance,
  ActivityProgress,
  Overhaul,
  MaintenanceCompliance,
  MaintenanceIssue,
  MaintenanceScheduleSummary,
  MaintenanceWindow,
} from './maintenance-plan';

export {
  DEFAULT_MAINTENANCE_PLANS,
  getDefaultMaintenancePlan,
  estimateMaintenanceCost,
  calculateMaintenanceWindow,
} from './maintenance-plan';

// Run assignment types
export type {
  Route,
  RunAssignment,
  DailySchedule,
  TrainRecommendation,
  AssignmentOptimizationRequest,
  AssignmentOptimizationResult,
  AssignmentWarning,
  MileageDeviation,
  FleetDailySummary,
  OpsMaintenanceMessage,
} from './run-assignment';

export {
  DEFAULT_ROUTES,
  calculateTrainScore,
  getMileageDeviationStatus,
} from './run-assignment';

// Resource and cost types
export type {
  ManHourAllocation,
  ManHourConfig,
  WorkshopTrack,
  TrackSlot,
  TrackUtilization,
  SparePart as SparePartResource,
  SparePartConsumption,
  SparePartForecast,
  CostBaseline,
  CostProjection,
  CostCompliance,
  LongTermResourcePlan,
  AnnualResourcePlan,
  ResourceLevelingResult,
} from './resource';

export {
  DEFAULT_MANHOUR_CONFIG,
  DEFAULT_WORKSHOP_TRACKS,
  getUtilizationStatus,
  getCostComplianceStatus,
  calculateLevelingScore,
} from './resource';

// Simulation types
export type {
  MileageStrategy,
  ScenarioParameter,
  Scenario,
  SimulationResult,
  SimulationMetrics,
  SimulationWarning,
  ScenarioComparison,
  SensitivityAnalysis,
  MultiScenarioAnalysis,
} from './simulation';

export {
  DEFAULT_SCENARIO_PARAMETERS,
  PRESET_SCENARIOS,
  calculateOverallScore,
  dominates,
} from './simulation';

// Man-Hour Projection Chart types (RFQ Section 8)
export type {
  ChartPeriod,
  ManHourDataPoint,
  GapSeverity,
  ComparisonMode,
  FleetFilter,
  TimeRange,
  ManHourChartConfig,
  ManHourChartState,
  ChartTooltipData,
  QuickRange,
  ChartDimensions,
  DataTableColumn,
  ChartEvents,
  ManHourSummary,
} from './man-hour-chart';

export {
  GAP_THRESHOLDS,
  classifyGap,
  getGapColor,
  DEFAULT_CHART_CONFIG,
  INITIAL_CHART_STATE,
  QUICK_RANGE_MONTHS,
  CHART_COLORS,
  CHART_DIMENSIONS,
  DEFAULT_TABLE_COLUMNS,
  formatPeriodLabel,
  calculateUtilization,
  createEmptyDataPoint,
  generatePeriods,
  calculateSummary,
} from './man-hour-chart';

// Dashboard types (RFQ Section 5 - Centralised Dashboard)
export type {
  KPIState,
  TrendDirection,
  KPIMetricId,
  KPICardData,
  KPIThresholds,
  FleetStatusSummary,
  TrainStatusRow,
  AlertSeverity,
  AlertCategory,
  DashboardAlert,
  AlertAction,
  PMTimelineMarker,
  TimelineConfig,
  AssignmentStatus,
  RunAssignmentRow,
  DailyAssignmentSummary,
  DashboardRole,
  DashboardViewMode,
  WidgetId,
  WidgetConfig,
  DashboardLayout,
  DashboardState,
} from './dashboard';

export {
  ALERT_STYLES,
  PM_LEVEL_COLORS,
  DASHBOARD_COLORS,
  BREAKPOINTS,
  GRID_CONFIG,
  getKPIState,
  formatKPIValue,
  getTrendDirection,
  calculateStatusPercentages,
  getDefaultWidgetsForRole,
} from './dashboard';

// Train Mileage Grid types (MILEAGE-UX-004)
export type {
  TrainGridColumnId,
  ColumnFilterType,

export {

} from './train-grid';

// Run Assignment UI types (MILEAGE-UX-005)
export type {
  RunAssignmentView,

  RouteSlot,
  DragTrainItem,
  DropResult,
  AISuggestionState,
  AISuggestion,
  ScoreFactor,
  ScoreBreakdown,
  BulkAcceptSummary,
  BalanceScoreLevel,
  FleetMileageBalance,
  DailyMileageBalance,
  WeeklyCellState,
  WeeklyGridCell,
  WeeklyPlannerData,
  TrainWeekView,
  TrainDayStatus,
  MessageFilters,
  MessageDraft,
  ToastType,
  ToastNotification,
  DailyScheduleViewState,

  MessagesViewState,

  AssignmentAction,
  UndoEntry,
  OverrideConfirmation,

  ScoreRange,
} from './run-assignment-ui';

export {
  DEFAULT_TRAIN_POOL_FILTERS,
  DEFAULT_TRAIN_POOL_SORT,
  BALANCE_SCORE_THRESHOLDS,

  getBalanceScoreLevel,

  getScoreRange,
  getScoreColor,
  PRIORITY_COLORS,
  FITNESS_STATUS_COLORS,
  formatAssignmentMileage,
  calculateFleetOverview,
  filterTrainPool,
  sortTrainPool,
  canAssignTrainToRoute,
  createToast,
} from './run-assignment-ui';

// Compliance Dashboard UI types (MILEAGE-UX-006)
// Note: TrendDirection is already exported from dashboard.ts
export type {
  ComplianceViewTab,
  CompliancePeriodType,
  CompliancePeriod,
  ComplianceStatus,
  BudgetStatus,
  ComplianceThresholds,
  ComplianceKPICard,
  ExecutiveSummaryKPIs,
  PMComplianceData,
  BacklogStatus,
  OverdueMaintenanceItem,
  BudgetActualSummary,
  CostCategoryBreakdown,
  WaterfallDataPoint,
  FleetCostComparison,
  CostTrendPoint,
  MileageComplianceData,
  MileageTrendPoint,

} from './compliance-ui';

export {
  DEFAULT_COMPLIANCE_THRESHOLDS,
  COMPLIANCE_STATUS_COLORS,
  getComplianceStatus,
  DEVIATION_SEVERITY_COLORS,
  getDeviationSeverity,

  formatCurrency,
  formatVariance,
  formatPercentage,
  calculateCompliancePercentage,
  getBudgetStatus,
  buildWaterfallData,
  generatePeriodLabel,
  createDefaultKPICard,
  calculateOverallCompliance,
} from './compliance-ui';

// What-If Scenario UI types (MILEAGE-UX-007)
export type {
  ScenarioStatus,
  ScenarioStatusConfig,
  ScenarioStrategyType,
  ScenarioTemplate,
  ScenarioParameters,
  ParameterSliderConfig,

export {

Now I have enough information to create a comprehensive frontend architecture plan. Let me compile the architectural design.

Based on my thorough analysis of the codebase, UX specifications, and research on PatternFly 6 with React/TypeScript/Vite best practices, here is the comprehensive frontend architecture plan for the Rolling Stock Mileage Management System.

---

# Frontend Architecture Plan: Rolling Stock Mileage Management System

## Executive Summary

This plan outlines the frontend architecture for a React + TypeScript + Vite application using PatternFly 6 design system. The system manages 128 trains (49 Kinki Sharyo + 79 Alstom) across 9 major screens with full mobile responsiveness. Development follows Storybook-first methodology with E2E testing via Playwright MCP.

---

## 1. Project Folder Structure

```
frontend/
├── .storybook/                     # Storybook 8 configuration
│   ├── main.ts                     # Storybook config
│   ├── preview.ts                  # Global decorators, PatternFly theme
│   └── manager.ts                  # Storybook UI customization
│
├── public/
│   ├── icons/                      # PWA icons (192, 512)
│   ├── manifest.json               # PWA manifest
│   └── favicon.ico
│
├── src/
│   ├── assets/                     # Static assets
│   │   ├── images/
│   │   └── fonts/
│   │
│   ├── components/                 # Atomic Design Component Library
│   │   ├── atoms/                  # Smallest building blocks
│   │   │   ├── Button/
│   │   │   │   ├── Button.tsx
│   │   │   │   ├── Button.stories.tsx
│   │   │   │   ├── Button.test.tsx
│   │   │   │   └── index.ts
│   │   │   ├── Badge/
│   │   │   ├── Icon/
│   │   │   ├── Spinner/
│   │   │   ├── ProgressBar/
│   │   │   ├── StatusChip/
│   │   │   ├── Tooltip/
│   │   │   └── index.ts
│   │   │
│   │   ├── molecules/              # Combinations of atoms
│   │   │   ├── KPICard/
│   │   │   ├── TrainCard/
│   │   │   ├── AlertCard/
│   │   │   ├── FilterChip/
│   │   │   ├── SearchInput/
│   │   │   ├── DateRangePicker/
│   │   │   ├── TimelineMarker/
│   │   │   ├── ScoreBreakdown/
│   │   │   └── index.ts
│   │   │
│   │   ├── organisms/              # Complex UI sections
│   │   │   ├── FleetStatusPanel/
│   │   │   ├── PMTimeline/
│   │   │   ├── TrainGrid/
│   │   │   ├── ManHourChart/

│   │   │   ├── NotificationCenter/
│   │   │   └── index.ts
│   │   │
│   │   ├── templates/              # Page layouts
│   │   │   ├── DashboardLayout/
│   │   │   ├── GridPageLayout/
│   │   │   ├── DetailPageLayout/
│   │   │   ├── MobileLayout/
│   │   │   └── index.ts
│   │   │
│   │   └── index.ts                # Re-export all components
│   │
│   ├── features/                   # Feature-based organization
│   │   ├── dashboard/              # MILEAGE-UX-003
│   │   │   ├── components/
│   │   │   │   ├── DashboardKPIs.tsx
│   │   │   │   ├── FleetOverview.tsx
│   │   │   │   └── QuickActions.tsx
│   │   │   ├── hooks/

│   │   │   ├── pages/
│   │   │   │   └── DashboardPage.tsx
│   │   │   └── index.ts
│   │   │
│   │   ├── trains/                 # MILEAGE-UX-004
│   │   │   ├── components/
│   │   │   │   ├── TrainGridView.tsx

│   │   │   │   ├── TrainFilters.tsx
│   │   │   │   └── TrainDetail.tsx
│   │   │   ├── hooks/

│   │   │   ├── pages/
│   │   │   │   ├── TrainListPage.tsx

│   │   │   └── index.ts
│   │   │

│   │   │   ├── components/
│   │   │   │   ├── ManHourProjectionChart.tsx

│   │   │   │   └── ManHourProjectionPage.tsx
```

## 2. Component Hierarchy (Atomic Design)

### 2.1 Atoms (Base Components)

Wrap PatternFly components with project-specific defaults:

| Atom | PatternFly Base | Purpose |
|------|-----------------|---------|
| `Button` | `Button` | Primary, Secondary, Danger variants |
| `Badge` | `Badge` | Notification counts |
| `StatusChip` | `Label` | Train status, fitness status |
| `ProgressBar` | `Progress` | PM window visualization |
| `Icon` | `@patternfly/react-icons` | Consistent icon usage |
| `Spinner` | `Spinner` | Loading states |
| `Tooltip` | `Tooltip` | Contextual information |

### 2.2 Molecules (Composed Components)

| Molecule | Composition | Screen Usage |
|----------|-------------|--------------|
| `KPICard` | Card + ProgressBar + Tooltip | Dashboard, Compliance |
| `TrainCard` | Card + StatusChip + Badge | Train Grid (mobile) |
| `AlertCard` | Card + Icon + Button | Alerts Panel |
| `FilterChip` | Label + CloseButton | All filter bars |
| `SearchInput` | TextInput + Icon | Grid toolbar |
| `DateRangePicker` | DatePicker + DatePicker | Reports, Scenarios |
| `TimelineMarker` | Tooltip + Icon | PM Timeline |
| `ScoreBreakdown` | DescriptionList | AI Recommendations |

### 2.3 Organisms (Complex Sections)

| Organism | Dependencies | Screen |
|----------|--------------|--------|
| `FleetStatusPanel` | KPICard, ProgressBar | Dashboard |
| `PMTimeline` | TimelineMarker, DateRangePicker | Dashboard, Schedule |
| `TrainGrid` | TanStack Table + PatternFly Table | Train Mileage Grid |
| `ManHourChart` | PatternFly ChartArea/ChartLine | Man-Hour Projection |
| `AlertsPanel` | AlertCard, Toolbar | Dashboard, Notifications |
| `AssignmentBoard` | DnD context + RouteSlots + TrainPool | Run Assignment |
| `ScenarioBuilder` | Slider, Form, Button | What-If Scenarios |
| `ReportGenerator` | Form, FileUpload, Table | Reports |
| `NotificationCenter` | AlertCard, Drawer, Tabs | Header, Page |

### 2.4 Templates (Page Layouts)

| Template | Layout | Screens |
|----------|--------|---------|
| `DashboardLayout` | 12-column grid with KPI row | Dashboard |
| `GridPageLayout` | Toolbar + Table + Pagination | Trains, Reports |
| `DetailPageLayout` | Breadcrumb + Tabs + Content | Train Detail |
| `MobileLayout` | Bottom tabs + Content | All (mobile) |

## 3. State Management Approach

### 3.1 Server State (TanStack Query)

Use TanStack Query for all API data:

```typescript
// src/lib/api/client.ts
import { QueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query';

export const queryClient = new QueryClient({
  defaultOptions: {
    queries: {
      staleTime: 5 * 60 * 1000,      // 5 minutes
      gcTime: 10 * 60 * 1000,        // 10 minutes (formerly cacheTime)
      retry: 2,
      refetchOnWindowFocus: false,
    },
  },
});

// Query key factory pattern
export const queryKeys = {
  trains: {
    all: ['trains'] as const,
    list: (filters: TrainGridFilters) => ['trains', 'list', filters] as const,
    detail: (id: string) => ['trains', 'detail', id] as const,
  },
  mileage: {
    daily: (date: string) => ['mileage', 'daily', date] as const,
    history: (trainId: string) => ['mileage', 'history', trainId] as const,
  },
  // ... other domains
};
```

### 3.2 Client State (Zustand)

Use Zustand for global UI state:

```typescript
// src/stores/useAppStore.ts
import { create } from 'zustand';
import { persist } from 'zustand/middleware';

interface AppState {
  // User preferences
  theme: 'light' | 'dark';
  role: DashboardRole;
  sidebarCollapsed: boolean;
  
  // UI state
  selectedFleet: FleetFilterOption;
  dateRange: { start: Date; end: Date };
  
  // Actions
  setTheme: (theme: 'light' | 'dark') => void;
  setRole: (role: DashboardRole) => void;
  toggleSidebar: () => void;
}

export const useAppStore = create<AppState>()(
  persist(
    (set) => ({
      theme: 'light',
      role: 'fleet_manager',
      sidebarCollapsed: false,
      selectedFleet: 'ALL',
      dateRange: { start: new Date(), end: new Date() },
      
      setTheme: (theme) => set({ theme }),
      setRole: (role) => set({ role }),
      toggleSidebar: () => set((s) => ({ sidebarCollapsed: !s.sidebarCollapsed })),
    }),
    { name: 'rsmm-app-store' }
  )
);
```

### 3.3 Local Component State

Use React `useState` and `useReducer` for component-specific state:

- Form inputs
- Modal visibility
- Selection state
- Pagination

## 4. Mock Data Strategy for Storybook

### 4.1 MSW (Mock Service Worker) Integration

```typescript
// src/lib/mock/handlers.ts
import { http, HttpResponse } from 'msw';
import { mockTrains, mockRoutes, mockMileageRecords } from './data';

export const handlers = [
  // GET /api/trains
  http.get('/api/trains', ({ request }) => {
    const url = new URL(request.url);
    const fleet = url.searchParams.get('fleet');
    
    let trains = mockTrains;
    if (fleet && fleet !== 'ALL') {
      trains = trains.filter(t => t.fleetType === fleet);
    }
    
    return HttpResponse.json({ data: trains, total: trains.length });
  }),
  
  // GET /api/trains/:id
  http.get('/api/trains/:id', ({ params }) => {
    const train = mockTrains.find(t => t.id === params.id);
    if (!train) {
      return HttpResponse.json({ error: 'Not found' }, { status: 404 });
    }
    return HttpResponse.json({ data: train });
  }),
  
  // ... other handlers
];
```

### 4.2 Mock Data Generation

Reuse generators from existing test fixtures:

```typescript
// src/lib/mock/data/trains.ts
import { 
  FleetType, 
  Train, 
  generateTrainId 
} from '@/mileage/types';

export function generateMockFleet(): Train[] {
  const trains: Train[] = [];
  
  // 49 Kinki Sharyo
  for (let i = 1; i <= 49; i++) {
    trains.push(createMockTrain('KINKI_SHARYO', i));
  }
  
  // 79 Alstom
  for (let i = 1; i <= 79; i++) {
    trains.push(createMockTrain('ALSTOM', i));
  }
  
  return trains;
}

function createMockTrain(fleet: FleetType, num: number): Train {
  const baseKm = 30000 + Math.random() * 20000;
  return {
    id: generateTrainId(fleet, num),
    name: `${fleet === 'KINKI_SHARYO' ? 'KS' : 'AL'}-${String(num).padStart(3, '0')}`,
    fleetType: fleet,
    currentMileage: Math.floor(baseKm),
    lifetimeMileage: Math.floor(baseKm * 10),
    status: 'IN_SERVICE',
    fitnessStatus: 'FIT',
    // ... other fields
  };
}

export const mockTrains = generateMockFleet();
```

### 6.2 PatternFly Component Customization

```typescript
// src/components/atoms/StatusChip/StatusChip.tsx
import { Label, LabelProps } from '@patternfly/react-core';
import type { PMWindowStatus } from '@/mileage/types';

const STATUS_COLORS: Record<PMWindowStatus, LabelProps['color']> = {
  SAFE: 'green',
  APPROACHING: 'orange',
  CRITICAL: 'red',
  OVERDUE: 'red',
};

interface StatusChipProps {
  status: PMWindowStatus;
  children: React.ReactNode;
}

export function StatusChip({ status, children }: StatusChipProps) {
  return (
    <Label 
      color={STATUS_COLORS[status]}
      isCompact={status !== 'OVERDUE'}
      icon={status === 'OVERDUE' ? <ExclamationCircleIcon /> : undefined}
    >
      {children}
    </Label>
  );
}
```

## 7. Implementation Sequence

### Phase 1: Foundation (Week 1-2)

1. **Project Setup**
   - Initialize Vite + React + TypeScript
   - Install PatternFly 6 packages
   - Configure Storybook 8
   - Set up MSW for mock data
   - Configure TanStack Query

2. **Core Components (Atoms)**
   - Button, Badge, Icon, Spinner
   - ProgressBar, StatusChip, Tooltip
   - Build Storybook stories for each

3. **Layout System**
   - AppLayout with PatternFly Page/Masthead/Sidebar
   - MobileLayout with bottom tabs
   - Breakpoint detection hook

### Phase 2: Dashboard (Week 3-4)

4. **Dashboard Components**
   - KPICard molecule
   - FleetStatusPanel organism
   - AlertsPanel organism
   - PMTimeline organism

5. **Dashboard Page**
   - DashboardPage assembly
   - Role-based widget configuration
   - Real-time data refresh

### Phase 3: Train Grid (Week 5-6)

6. **Grid Components**
   - TrainGrid with TanStack Table
   - PM Window progress cell
   - Filter bar and chips
   - Mobile card view

7. **Grid Features**
   - Sorting and filtering
   - Bulk actions
   - Virtual scrolling (128 rows)
   - Export functionality

### Phase 4: Man-Hour Chart (Week 7)

8. **Chart Components**
   - ManHourProjectionChart
   - GapAnalysisTable
   - Timeline controls

9. **Chart Features**
   - Comparison modes (Without/With adjustment)
   - Zoom levels
   - Data table integration

### Phase 5: Run Assignment (Week 8-9)

10. **Assignment Components**
    - TrainPool with filters
    - AssignmentBoard (DnD)
    - AIRecommendations panel
    - WeeklyPlanner grid

11. **Assignment Features**
    - Drag-and-drop
    - Score breakdown tooltips
    - Bulk accept/reject
    - Keyboard accessibility

### Phase 6: Remaining Screens (Week 10-12)

12. **Compliance Dashboard**
13. **What-If Scenarios**
14. **Report Generation**
15. **Alert/Notification System**
16. **Mobile Optimization**

## 9. Unknown Unknowns (Risks to Address)

1. **PatternFly 6 + TanStack Table Integration**: No official guide - need custom cell renderers
2. **Offline PWA with TanStack Query**: Persisted cache strategy complexity
3. **Drag-and-Drop Accessibility**: Keyboard navigation for assignment board
4. **Victory Charts Performance**: Large datasets (144 months x 3 series)
5. **Mobile Touch Targets**: PatternFly components may need overrides
6. **Dark Mode Consistency**: PatternFly + custom colors alignment
7. **Bundle Size**: PatternFly tree-shaking effectiveness
